data.title: Sierra Vista, Ariz., to Sell $16.1 Million in Education Facility Revenue Bonds
data.description:
By Chris Wack

The Industrial Development Authority of the City of Sierra Vista in Arizona plans to sell $16.1 million of Series 2026 education facility revenue bonds to loan to Desert Star Academy, an Arizona non-profit corporation.

Desert Star Academy intends to use the proceeds to finance or refinance the purchase, construction, improvement, renovation and equipping of land and charter school facilities located in Fort Mohave, Ariz., according to documents posted on MuniOS.

Interest on the bonds is payable semiannually on each June 15 and Dec. 15. Rates and yields haven't yet been determined.

The bonds are special limited obligations of the Industrial Development Authority.

The authority is a non-profit corporation designated a political subdivision of the state of Arizona, and isn't pledging its general credit for the bonds.

Desert Star Academy is an organization operated exclusively for charitable and educational purposes as a charter school under Arizona laws.

Some of the proceeds will be used for constructing a 30,000-square-foot, two-story pre-engineered metal building for high school classrooms and a sports stadium. The project is expected to cost about $12.1 million, and be substantially completed by March 2027.

Hilltop Securities is listed as senior managers for the sale.

Write to Chris Wack at chris.wack@wsj.com

data.title: Mining giant Grupo Mexico's net profit doubles in fourth quarter on metals boom
data.description:
Grupo Mexico , one of the world's largest copper producers, nearly doubled its fourth-quarter net profit on higher metals prices and stronger volumes, it said on Tuesday, more than offsetting weaker infrastructure results tied to suspended oil platforms and foreign-exchange headwinds.

The conglomerate, which last year became the largest listed company on the Mexican market, saw net profits reach $1.43 billion, above the $1.32 billion estimate from analysts polled by LSEG.

Earnings were boosted by increased production, with copper output climbing 2% and gold jumping 14%. Prices also leaped, with copper up 22%, silver up 74% and gold up 56%, Grupo Mexico said.

Revenues for the group climbed 34% to $5.15 billion, above analysts' $4.87 billion estimate.

The miner is eyeing billions of dollar in investments over the next decade, with projects pending at home and in the U.S., Peru and Spain.

Grupo Mexico said its U.S. unit, Asarco, could double mine production due to new copper policies outlined by President Donald Trump. It added that it was moving forward with an assessment to reopen and expand its Hayden smelter and Amarillo refinery in the U.S.

In Mexico, the firm said it is in talks with the current administration to resume more than $10 billion in investments after permits were paused under the previous government.

Grupo Mexico's infrastructure division swung to a net loss in the quarter, weighing down results, pressured by the suspension of four of offshore oil platforms by state-owned producer Pemex (PEMX.UL) and foreign-exchange effects.

data.title: Stride rises after upbeat Q2 results
data.description:
** Shares of edutech firm Stride up 31.5% at $95.25 after the bell

** Co's Q2 revenue rises 7.5% YoY to $631.3 million vs analysts' avg. estimate of $627.9 million — data compiled by LSEG

** Q2 enrollments up 7.8%; revenue per enrollment up 1.8%

** Q2 adjusted earnings per share of $2.50 beats estimates of $2.08 apiece

** Co expects annual adjusted operating income of $485 million to $505 million, up from $475 million to $500 million

** For Q3, LRN expects revenue between $615 million and $645 million vs estimates of $613.6 million

** LRN sees adjusted operating income in the range of $130 million to $140 million for Q3

** Stock fell 37.5% in 2025

data.title: JGB Futures Edge Higher; Focus on 40-Year Auction — Market Talk
data.description: JGB futures edge higher in the morning Tokyo session, but price movements may be constrained ahead of the Japanese Finance Ministry's auction today of about 400 billion yen of 40-year sovereign securities. "There may be strong short-covering demand from dealers at the auction, but we think investors are unlikely to chase any rally," Citi Research's Tomohisa Fujiki says in a research report. "Demand from pension funds may be smaller than a week ago as both equities and the USDJPY have declined," the rates strategist adds. The 10-year JGB futures are 0.08 yen higher at 131.31 yen. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Australian shares climb as commodities lift gold, oil stocks ahead of key inflation data
data.description:
Australian shares advanced on Wednesday, buoyed by gains in gold and oil stocks on firmer commodity prices, while investors awaited a critical inflation report expected to guide the central bank's next monetary policy decision.

The S&P/ASX 200 index rose 0.4% to 8,976.40 by 2335 GMT and is set for its fourth consecutive session of gains. The benchmark advanced 0.9% to hit a three-month high on Tuesday.

A robust jobs report last week has sharpened expectations of an early interest-rate hike, placing added weight on consumer price data, due later in the day, to gauge the Reserve Bank of Australia's (RBA) policy path.

Markets are focused on the December-quarter trimmed-mean inflation, expected to rise 0.8%, which would lift the annual rate to 3.3%, above the RBA's 2%-3% target band.

An increase of 0.7% or less would temper the odds of a rate hike at the RBA's February 3 meeting, while a 0.9% or higher print would sharply swing the chances toward tightening, leaving a 0.8% increase to go down to the wire.

Traders are pricing in a near 60% chance of a quarter-point rise to the cash rate next week, with positions set to shift on the inflation print.

Energy firms leapt 1.4% to hit their highest since September 3 after oil futures surged. Woodside Energy rose 1.8% after the oil giant's $3.04 billion quarterly revenue topped Visible Alpha's $2.84 billion estimate.

Gold stocks (.AXDG) rose 1% after the bullion jumped to a record peak. Sub-index heavyweight Northern Star Resources climbed as much as 2.5%.

Miners jumped 1.3% to a second straight session of record high. Mining giants BHP , Rio Tinto and Fortescue rose between 0.4% and 1.5%.

Financials edged up 0.2%, with top lender Commonwealth Bank of Australia advancing 0.4%.

Meanwhile, New Zealand's benchmark S&P/NZX 50 index inched down 0.1% to 13,499.23.

data.title: Rockland Resources Closes Final Private Placement Tranche
data.description:
(TheNewswire)

    Vancouver, British Columbia, January 27, 2026 –TheNewswire - RocklandResources Ltd. (the “Company” or "Rockland")(CSE: RKL), is pleased toannounce that further to its press releases dated January 9, 2026 andJanuary 21, 2026, the Company has closed the final tranche of thenon-brokered private placement. The Company issued 1,120,000 units(the "Units") at a price of $0.10 per Unit for aggregategross proceeds of $112,000. Each Unit is comprised of one common share("Share") and one transferable common share purchase warrantof the Company ("Warrant").  Each Warrant will entitle the
    Subscriber to purchase one Warrant Share for a 36-month period after
    the Closing Date at an exercise price of $0.15 per share. Finders’ fees of $2,000 cash were paid in connection with
    the closing of the final tranche.


    With the closing of this final tranche, the Company has
    completed the financing announced January 9, 2026, issuing a total of
    12,000,000 Units for aggregate gross proceeds of $1,200,000. Total
    finders’ fees of $7,800 were paid. Proceeds raised will be used to
    advance the Company’s Cole Gold Mines project in Red Lake, Ontario
    as well as for general working capital.  


    Shares issued pursuant to the Financing will be subject
    to a four-month hold period according to applicable securities laws
    of Canada.


    About Rockland Resources Ltd.


    Rockland Resources is engaged in the business of
    mineral exploration and the acquisition of mineral property assets for
    the benefit of its shareholders.


    On Behalf of the Board of
    Directors


    Michael England, CEO & Director


    For further information, please
    contact:


    Mike England


    Email:  mike@engcom.ca

    Neither the Canadian SecuritiesExchange nor itsRegulation Services Provider accepts responsibility for the adequacy or accuracy of this release.

    Copyright (c) 2026 TheNewswire - All rights reserved.

data.title: Key facts: XRP Drops Below $2; Santiment Calls It Undervalued; Ripple Partners in Saudi Arabia
data.description:
• XRP (XRPUSD) dropped below $2, with rising open interest and negative funding rates indicating leveraged traders' influence. Spot volume increased as XRP neared a key support level at $1.8.1
  • Santiment reports XRP is 'undervalued' with a 30-day MVRV Ratio of -5.7%, showing recent buyers are facing losses.2
  • Ripple partners with Jeel, Riyad Bank's innovation unit, to develop blockchain solutions in Saudi Arabia, targeting cross-border payments, digital asset custody, and tokenization under Vision 2030.3

data.title: Key facts: Tether acquires 27 tons of gold; USDT listed on major platforms; man sentenced for $37M laundering
data.description:
• Tether, the issuer of the USDT stablecoin, announced the purchase of 27 tons of gold in the last quarter of 2025 to boost its reserves.1
  • Tether (USDTUSD) is now available on platforms like Bybit, Crypto.com, Kraken, OKX, and Moonpay, issued by Anchorage Digital Bank with Cantor Fitzgerald as the reserve custodian.2
  • Jingliang Su, 45, received nearly four years in prison for laundering about $37 million in illegal digital assets, converted to Tether (USDT) and sent to scam leaders in Cambodia.3

data.title: Key facts: Chainlink Trading at $11.98; Joins KRW Stablecoin Alliance
data.description:
• Chainlink (LINKUSD) is trading at $11.98, up 1.1% in 24 hours but down 5.4% this week. Support is noted at $7.15, potentially limiting further declines.1
  • Chainlink has joined the Global Alliance for KRW Stablecoin to aid in developing KRW stablecoin standards in Korea, enhancing data and oracle infrastructure for compliance and reliability.23

data.title: Key facts: Optimism to Vote on OP Token Proposal; Unlocks 31M Tokens; Enhances Quantum Resistance
data.description:
• On January 27-28, 2026, Optimism governance will hold a 25-hour vote on a proposal to align the OP token with long-term goals, impacting distribution and governance.1
  • On January 31, Optimism will unlock 31,340,000 OP tokens, accounting for approximately 1.62% of the current circulating supply.2
  • Optimism is gearing up for a post-quantum future by enhancing its OP Stack with pluggable post-quantum signature schemes, mirroring Ethereum's strategy for quantum resistance.3

data.title: Key facts: MicroStrategy acquires 2,932 Bitcoin; Animoca Brands develops DeFi tools; Bitcoin price drop worsens
data.description:
• MicroStrategy purchased an additional 2,932 Bitcoin at an average price of $90,061, raising its total Bitcoin holdings to 712,647 BTC.1
  • Animoca Brands Japan and RootstockLabs are creating Bitcoin-based DeFi tools for Japanese firms, enhancing treasury efficiency and integrating Bitcoin into corporate financial strategies.2
  • Bitcoin's price drop was worsened by forced liquidations in the derivatives market, impacting traders expecting gains in 2026. A decline in Open Interest shows reduced leverage before the fall.3

data.title: Key facts: Ethereum Surges Above $2,900; BitMine Acquires 40,302 ETH; Onchain Activity Hits 2.78M Transactions
data.description:
• Ethereum tested $2,800, now trading above $2,900. A break above $2,910 could lead to gains, while staying above $2,880 is crucial after a dip to $2,780.1
  • BitMine Immersion Technologies acquired 40,302 ETH, raising its total to 4,243,338 ETH, or 3.52% of the supply. The company staked 171,264 ETH, totaling over 2 million ETH.23
  • Ethereum (ETHUSD) saw a surge in onchain activity, with daily transactions hitting 2.78 million and a 50% rise in active addresses. Transaction fees are at nine-year lows.4

data.title: BXP forecasts 2026 funds from operations below estimates amid industry-wide leasing slump
data.description:
Real estate investment trust BXP , formerly known as Boston Properties, on Tuesday forecast annual funds from operations below Wall Street estimates, as the industry grapples with weak leasing demand amid broader macroeconomic uncertainty.

The U.S. administration's changing trade policies have led to uncertainty among companies, delaying capital investments and workforce expansion.

The Boston, Massachusetts-based company now sees 2026 funds from operations — a key measure of a REIT's performance — between $6.88 and $7.04 per share, the midpoint of which is below the analysts' average estimate of $7.00 per share, according to data compiled by LSEG.

It expects first-quarter FFO between $1.56 and $1.58 per share.

The company reported FFO of $1.76 per share for the quarter ended December 31, compared with $1.79 per share a year earlier.

Its revenue from leasing rose 1.4% to $809.15 million in the fourth quarter from $798.19 million in the prior year.

BXP reported net income of $248.49 million, or $1.56 per share, from a loss of $230.02 million, or $1.45 per share a year earlier.

Its fourth-quarter revenue rose 1.37% to $877.10 million, beating estimates of $864.66 million, according to data compiled by LSEG.
